title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Poirot investigates the second-floor room where Colonel Potter was found dead in an armchair with a gunshot wound to his right temple. Greves notes that the death was assumed to have occurred a few hours prior, though no one heard the shot. Poirot observes that the room is orderly, but finds it highly unusual that a meticulous man like Potter left no suicide note or letter. The landlady, encountered on the stairs, provides a lengthy account, claiming Potter had been depressed and pacing following a coroner's inquest in Wormsley Valley regarding the murder of a friend. She mentions he had been struggling with money and refused food.

Later, Poirot meets Francis Claude at Stagg. Francis confesses that she is related to the man posing as Inoke Yates, identifying him as her distant cousin, Charles. She reveals that her family was in desperate need of money because her husband faced financial ruin and potential imprisonment. Francis admits that she conceived a plan to blackmail David Hunter by exploiting a rumor that Rosaline Claude's first husband might still be alive, which would invalidate her inheritance. 

Francis explains that she recruited Charles to impersonate "Robert Andrae" to facilitate the scheme. While the plan initially worked and Hunter agreed to deliver money, the situation turned violent. Francis states that Charles was murdered by the "dangerous" David Hunter. Poirot concludes by questioning if Francis then manipulated Colonel Potter into identifying Charles as "Robert Andrich" to further her objectives.
